steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question. Text 1 presents Ramanathan et al.'s finding that K2-18 b sits on the inner edge of the habitable zone (HZ) at about 1 AU. Text 2 presents Chen et al.'s competing claim that hydrogen-rich atmospheres let in light that raises surface temperatures, pushing the HZ inner edge out to 3.85 AU. The question asks how Chen et al. would most likely respond to Ramanathan et al. — specifically, what challenge Chen et al.'s work poses to Text 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "By arguing that K2-18 b and other hycean candidates are unlikely to support life because these planets are located too far from the stars they orbit.": Chen et al. say K2-18 b is too close (inside the revised HZ), not too far. This reverses the direction of the claim. ✗
  • "By stating that the chemical composition of the atmosphere of the hycean candidate Ramanathan et al. identified suggests that this planet's surface is unlikely to harbor liquid water.": Chen et al. argue that the hydrogen-rich atmosphere admits wavelengths that raise temperatures and evaporate water — so K2-18 b (at ~1 AU) falls inside the revised inner HZ edge of 3.85 AU and cannot retain liquid water. This accurately captures their response. ✓
  • "By maintaining that Ramanathan et al. relied on a model whose estimates of surface temperatures on hycean candidates are likely too high.": It is Chen et al. who find higher surface temperatures than earlier assessments; they do not accuse Ramanathan et al. of overestimating temperatures. ✗
  • "By observing that unlike the hycean candidate Ramanathan et al. discovered, most other types of planets with hydrogen-rich atmospheres are likely located within the HZ.": Text 2 makes no such comparative claim about other planet types. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
